author: bsky.app
alt_text:
We've increased the number of photos you can attach to a post from 4 to 10. That's why we're adding a carousel. | The carousel only shows for posts with at least 5 photos.
Old posts with photos will continue to show the regular grid layout. | Each photo displays a numbered badge to make it super clear there are more photos to browse. | We've improved the gesture handling so you don't accidentally open the drawer menu or go back/forward in your browser while swiping. | We're always eager to hear your feedback. Let us know your thoughts about the new carousel!

author: bsky.app
alt_text: Bluesky 1.99. Improvements: Videos are now up to 3 minutes long! A DMs "inbox" to approve or reject new direct messages. You can now mute accounts from a post's "..." menu. An improved moderation reporting flow. Better tablet-size layouts on Web. Better error reporting during signup.

alt_text: Bluesky 1.98. Improvements: You can now restrict replies to "Followers only". New settings page: Post Interaction Settings. Added "Search posts" to user profiles. Improvements to the search page. New translation: Interlingua

author: bsky.app
alt_text: Bluesky 1.96. Improvements: New "Mentions" tab in notifications. Usernames on .bsky.social are now reserved when you switch to your own domain. New button to change reply ordering and visual style in threads. Visual improvements to the app. New translations: Nepali, Khmer, and Romanian.

author: bsky.app
alt_text: Bluesky 1.95. Improvements: New reply sorting option: "hotness." Improved localizations. Smoother feed scrolling. You can now swipe away informational "toasts". Fixes: Camera photos no longer get stretched. Newlines no longer get dropped on copypaste. Lots of other bug fixes and performance improvements.

author: bsky.app
alt_text: Bluesky 1.94. Improvements: Redesigned settings screen. New animations when viewing images. Imported images now clearly indicate that they were imported. Fixes: Lots of performance improvements!

author: propublica.org
alt_text: Graphic displaying three men's photos, stacked on top of each other, in a column on the left. Text on right reads: Gerardo del Valle, Alejandro Bonilla Suarez and Edwin Corona Ramos. ProPublica, The Texas Tribune, Alianza Rebelde Investiga, and Cazadores de Fake News. Livingston Award, International Reporting.

author: propublica.org
alt_text: Graphic displaying how hundreds of staff who do work related to nuclear reactors and their safety have left and not been replaced. The data is categorized into six sections: Nuclear Reactor Regulation, Nuclear Material Safety and Safeguards, Nuclear Regulatory Research, Nuclear Security and Incident Response, Regional offices, and Other offices. Each orange square underneath a category represents a loss in staff, while each gray square represents an addition of staff. The graphic shows that there have been 443 losses and 57 arrivals across all six of these categories. Note: The data is from the week ending Jan. 24, 2025, through Feb. 13, 2026. Source: Weekly Information Reports from the Nuclear Regulatory Commission.

"I told my guy he'd probably be better off if he broke into the Capitol or made a major donation to Trump's inauguration." | Screenshot of John Oliver on Last Week Tonight. He is citing a ProPublica story dated March 30, 2026 with a headline that reads "A Nursing Home Owner Got a Trump Pardon. The Families of His Patients Got Nothing."
